content: The Relative Strength Index (RSI), developed by J. Welles Wilder, measures momentum on a 0-100 scale. Calculated as RSI = 100 - [100/(1+RS)] where RS = average gains/average losses over typically 14 periods. Readings above 70 suggest overbought conditions; below 30 suggest oversold. Divergence between RSI and price can signal potential reversals. However, RSI can remain overbought/oversold during strong trends.
